webhook_event.note: Webhook fired internally during this run despite Sinch API docs saying webhooks aren't exposed to API consumers; payload not visible through dashboard UI.

notes: Experiment 3a brand-registration leg, captured via Sinch dashboard not API. Two distinct state-tracking concepts captured: identity_status_observed (TCR-level brand identity field, terminal value VERIFIED only) and bundle_state_transitions (Sinch's bundle-level lifecycle, full sequence In review -> Approved). Sole Prop is not a supported brandEntityType in the Sinch 10DLC API enum (PUBLIC | PRIVATE | CHARITY_NON_PROFIT). HEALTHCARE is a valid brandVerticalType at the API level — D-18 healthcare decline is RelayKit policy, not carrier constraint. Three API-vs-dashboard inconsistencies observed: state machine (5 API states vs 7 dashboard states), Simplified pricing ($10 API docs vs $6 dashboard charged), webhook policy (API docs say poll-only but BRAND_IDENTITY_STATUS_UPDATE fired internally). All three flagged for Phase 2 Session B kickoff verification with Sinch BDR. PII redacted with placeholders ({IRS_ON_FILE_ADDRESS}, {CONTACT_DETAILS}); EIN value not stored in fixture. Brand BTTC6XS is live and available for the campaign-registration leg of Experiment 3 (Experiment 3b, BLOCKED on procedure drafting). Joel's IRS name change letter was sent 2026-04-24 and not yet processed at submission time; registration succeeded anyway — implication: legal-name verification may have grace windows in Sinch/TCR's flow.
